name: Build a Deck
description: Build a wood or composite deck attached to your house. One of the most rewarding DIY projects that adds real value to your home.
rent_instead: Post hole auger — $50/day, saves hours of digging
Miter saw — $45/day if you only need it for this project
common_mistakes: Not checking local building codes and permit requirements before starting
Skipping the ledger board flashing — leads to water damage
Using regular screws instead of structural screws for framing
Not pre-drilling hardwood decking — causes splitting
Setting posts too shallow — code usually requires 42 inch depth for frost line

name: Build a Fence
description: Install a wood privacy or picket fence around your yard. Great first outdoor project.
common_mistakes: Not calling 811 to mark underground utilities before digging
Not checking property lines — fences built over the line must be removed
Posts not deep enough — need to go below frost line
Not leaving a gap at the bottom for drainage and uneven ground
Using untreated wood for posts below ground

name: Bathroom Renovation
description: Full bathroom remodel including tile, fixtures, and plumbing. A complex project that transforms your space.
common_mistakes: Not addressing moisture and waterproofing behind tile
Cutting corners on cement board — drywall behind tile will fail
Forgetting to slope the shower floor toward the drain
Not testing plumbing before closing up walls
Underestimating the time for tiling — it takes longer than you think

name: Kitchen Renovation
description: Kitchen remodel including cabinets, countertops, and appliances. The most complex home renovation.
common_mistakes: Not planning the layout with the work triangle in mind
Forgetting to account for appliance dimensions before ordering cabinets
Not leveling cabinets — even 1/8 inch off shows on countertops
Doing demolition before having a plan for eating during construction
Underestimating budget by 20-30% — kitchens always cost more than expected

name: Finish a Basement
description: Frame, insulate, drywall, and finish a basement space. Adds significant living space to your home.
rent_instead: Drywall lift — $40/day, essential for ceiling panels
common_mistakes: Not addressing moisture issues before framing — test for dampness first
Forgetting to leave access to the sump pump and water shutoff
Not planning electrical before closing walls — moving outlets later is painful
Insulating with fiberglass without a vapor barrier
Not meeting code for egress windows in bedrooms

name: Install Hardwood/Laminate Flooring
description: Install hardwood or laminate flooring in one or more rooms. Dramatically transforms a space.
common_mistakes: Not acclimating the flooring to the room for 48-72 hours before install
Forgetting expansion gaps along walls — flooring needs room to expand
Not checking subfloor for levelness — humps and dips will show through
Starting from the wrong wall — start from the longest, most visible wall
Not staggering end joints — minimum 6 inches between rows

name: Install Tile
description: Install ceramic or porcelain tile on floors or walls. Patient work with beautiful results.
rent_instead: Wet tile saw — $60/day, essential for anything beyond basic cuts
common_mistakes: Not using the right thinset for the tile type — large format tile needs large-format mortar
Spreading too much thinset at once — it skins over and won't bond
Not back-buttering large tiles — leads to hollow spots
Grouting too soon — wait 24 hours after setting tile
Not sealing grout — unsealed grout stains permanently

name: Build Raised Garden Beds
description: Build simple raised garden beds from lumber. Perfect beginner project — done in a day.
common_mistakes: Using treated lumber that isn't rated for ground contact — use ground contact rated or cedar
Making beds too wide — keep under 4 feet so you can reach the center from either side
Not leveling the ground underneath first
Forgetting drainage — don't put a solid bottom in the bed

name: Build a Shed
description: Build a small to medium storage shed. A satisfying project that gives you useful outdoor storage.
rent_instead: Framing nailer — $50/day if you don't own one
common_mistakes: Not checking local setback requirements — sheds often need to be X feet from property line
Building on bare ground — use gravel pad or concrete blocks for foundation
Not squaring the foundation — measure diagonals, they must be equal
Skimping on roof sheathing — leads to sagging
Forgetting a drip edge on the roof

name: Drywall Installation & Finishing
description: Hang, tape, mud, and sand drywall. The skill is in the finishing — anyone can hang it.
rent_instead: Drywall lift — $40/day, saves your back and your marriage
common_mistakes: Not staggering seams — joints should not line up between rows
Over-driving screws — they should dimple, not break the paper
Applying mud too thick — multiple thin coats are better than one thick coat
Not feathering edges wide enough — sand marks show through paint
Sanding before the mud is fully dry — leads to gouging

name: Paint a Room
description: Prep and paint walls and trim in a room. The easiest way to transform a space.
rent_instead: Paint sprayer — $50/day for large areas or ceilings
common_mistakes: Skipping the prep — 80% of a good paint job is preparation
Not using primer — especially important on new drywall, dark colors, or stains
Removing painter's tape after paint is fully dry — pull it while still slightly tacky
Buying cheap brushes and rollers — good tools make a huge difference
Not maintaining a wet edge — leads to lap marks

name: Install Crown Molding / Trim
description: Install crown molding, baseboard, or trim. Adds a finished, polished look to any room.
common_mistakes: Not coping inside corners — miter joints on inside corners always open up
Not finding studs — brad nails alone won't hold crown molding
Cutting crown molding flat on the saw — it needs to be upside down and angled
Not accounting for out-of-square walls — test fit before nailing
Filling nail holes before painting — caulk the gaps, fill the holes

name: Basic Plumbing Repairs
description: Fix leaks, replace faucets, unclog drains. Essential homeowner skills that save expensive service calls.
rent_instead: Drain snake / auger — $30/day for tough clogs
common_mistakes: Not turning off the water supply before starting
Over-tightening connections — causes cracking in porcelain and plastic
Using the wrong type of Teflon tape or pipe compound
Forgetting to check for leaks after reassembly — run water for 5 minutes and inspect
Pouring chemical drain cleaners — they damage pipes. Use a snake instead

name: Basic Electrical Work
description: Replace outlets, switches, light fixtures. Know when to DIY and when to call an electrician.
common_mistakes: Not turning off the breaker AND verifying power is off with a tester
Working on circuits you don't understand — when in doubt, hire an electrician
Using the wrong wire gauge for the circuit amperage
Not securing wires properly in boxes — loose wires cause fires
Backstabbing outlets instead of using screw terminals — backstabs fail over time

name: Hang Shelves / Cabinets
description: Mount shelves or wall cabinets. Simple but satisfying — instant storage and organization.
common_mistakes: Not finding studs — drywall anchors alone can't hold heavy shelves
Not leveling — even 1/4 inch off is visible across a shelf
Drilling into plumbing or electrical — use a stud finder with wire detection
Mounting cabinets without a temporary ledger board for support

name: Assemble Furniture
description: Assemble flat-pack or kit furniture. Your drill is your best friend here.
common_mistakes: Not reading the instructions before starting — lay out all pieces first
Tightening screws fully before all pieces are connected — finger-tight first, then tighten
Using a power drill on cam locks — they strip easily, use a screwdriver
Assembling on carpet — move to a hard, flat surface

name: Car Maintenance Basics
description: Oil changes, brake pads, basic maintenance. Save hundreds per year doing it yourself.
rent_instead: Many auto parts stores lend specialty tools for free
safety_gear: Jack stands — never work under a car on a jack alone
common_mistakes: Over-torquing drain plugs and lug nuts — use a torque wrench
Not using jack stands — jacks fail, stands don't
Forgetting to pump the brake pedal after changing pads — no brakes on first push
Cross-threading lug nuts — hand-start them first, then torque in a star pattern

name: Yard Maintenance
description: Mowing, edging, trimming, leaf blowing. The weekly routine that keeps your yard looking sharp.
common_mistakes: Mowing too short — never cut more than 1/3 of the grass height at once
Mowing on the same pattern every week — alternate directions to prevent ruts
Not sharpening mower blades — dull blades tear grass, causing brown tips
Running string trimmer line too close to trees — it damages bark

name: Tree Trimming / Removal
description: Trim branches or remove small trees. Know your limits — large trees require professionals.
common_mistakes: Tackling trees too large for DIY — anything over 10 inches diameter, hire an arborist
Not planning the fall direction — trees are unpredictable
Cutting branches flush to the trunk — leave the branch collar for proper healing
Using a ladder with a chainsaw — extremely dangerous combination
Not wearing chainsaw chaps — they've saved countless legs

name: Concrete Work
description: Pour a small patio or walkway. Heavy work but incredibly satisfying when done right.
safety_gear: Long sleeves — wet concrete burns skin
common_mistakes: Not preparing a proper gravel base — 4 inches of compacted gravel minimum
Ordering too little concrete — always order 10% extra
Not having enough help — concrete waits for no one, you need 2-3 people minimum
Forgetting control joints — concrete will crack; control joints tell it where
Finishing too early or too late — wait for the bleed water to disappear
